differential_diagnoses: Non-Hodgkin lymphoma with reactive lymphoplasmacytic infiltrate
next_steps: Perform immunohistochemical staining for CD68, CD163, CD1a, and Langerin on existing and new biopsy tissue to distinguish histiocytic neoplasms
Test for BRAF V600E mutation and other MAPK pathway mutations in biopsy tissue
Review imaging for pathognomonic symmetric diaphyseal sclerosis of the long bones characteristic of Erdheim-Chester disease
Consult hematopathology/oncology for expert review of marrow-infiltrating histiocytic cells
Perform PET-CT or MRI of the long bones to look for characteristic medullary lesions
Monitor serum IgG4 levels and evaluate for other site involvement (e.g., orbits, aorta, pancreas) to clarify the relationship between IgG4-RD and potential underlying histiocytosis
